items.summary: The clean-up bill for the Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter site may ultimately may be paid by the Tasmanian government, according to legal experts, following administrators' failure to secure a buyer for the embattled operation.

items.title: Irish national admits role in 'nightmare' plan to help alleged criminal flee Australia
items.summary: After agreeing to help smuggle an alleged drug trafficker out of Australia, Stephen Roche found himself at the centre of a living "nightmare", stranded in the outback without funds or friends, court documents have revealed.

items.title: Harp therapist serenades lemurs, rhinos and fish at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium
items.summary: Harp therapist Alison Ware provides musical enrichment for animals at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium, adapting what she plays to suit different species.

items.title: Young Australians with bowel cancer say doctors missing the warning signs
items.summary: Georgie Cauchi had blood in her stool, constant pain and was struggling to get out of bed, but still in her 20s doctors told her she was too young to have bowel cancer. When it was finally discovered several years later, the tumour had grown so large it was blocking her entire bowel.

items.title: Victoria on high alert for signs of deadly H5N1 bird flu
items.summary: Victorians are on high alert for the deadly H5N1 bird flu, as citizen scientists help to monitor infected birds.

items.title: How a century-old town hall became the heart of a small Tasmanian community
items.summary: Every Sunday, a group of men and women get together for some American clogging, home-baked afternoon tea and community connection.

items.title: Winter weather brings new and unique shells to southern WA beaches
items.summary: WA's southern coastline is covered in seashells, much to the delight of collectors. However, the state has a bag limit on how many you can take home.

items.title: Attacking the media for asking legitimate question is straight out of Trump's playbook
items.summary: A minister of the Crown's scolding of a journalist asking a legitimate question about firearm ownership, and the subsequent pile-on allowed to run on social media, should ring alarm bells for anyone concerned with democracy.

items.title: Artist behind portrait in Epstein files reunites with collector who purchased first piece
items.summary: A high school art portrait launched Petrina Ryan-Kleid's career. Forty years and several satirical portraits later, she has been reunited with the person who bought her first work.

items.title: Queensland government crime statistics compare 'apples to oranges' amid timeframe inconsistency
items.summary: Queensland's premier says new statistics show crime is falling across the state, but his data sources are inconsistent.

items.title: Sub-Antarctic petrels spotted live off NSW coast for first time
items.summary: Two Southern Ocean deepwater seabirds, the Kerguelen petrel and the grey petrel, have been photographed off Port Macquarie, during a "stunningly rare" southern seabird event.

items.title: Platform connects city investors with farming across Australia via their smartphones
items.summary: An Australian investment platform is helping farmers raise capital by connecting them with everyday investors who can buy shares in crops and livestock while sharing the risks and rewards of farming.

items.title: Protests scrutinised as antisemitism royal commission focuses on university activism
items.summary: Universities have apologised to Jewish staff and students who have spoken of their distress at being targeted by protest movement.

items.title: Minister concedes big tech companies using Australian content to train AI models is 'unethical'
items.summary: Australian artists have already had their music taken to train overseas AI models without payment or consent.

items.title: Federal racism inquiry hears of racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence
items.summary: Racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence have taken a toll on First Nations people, a federal inquiry heard this week.

items.title: Prospects for Palestinian statehood 'disappearing', former world leaders say
items.summary: The two former world leaders are demanding the international community heap more pressure on Israel to change course.

items.title: Want to know who you'll marry? Science says you should look in the mirror
items.summary: Once I saw it, I couldn't unsee it. Had I noticed something real or was it just the people around me? I set out to find out what the data had to say.

items.title: From scream king to COVID dad: The on and off-screen faces of Sam Neill

items.title: One Nation's Malcolm Roberts falsely claimed COVID-19 vaccines caused miscarriages
items.summary: The One Nation senator made the previously unreported remarks during a live stream discussion with three prominent Australian anti-vaccine mandate activists.

items.title: Shortage of refined fuels threatening global economy
items.summary: There is a rapidly escalating supply crunch that has yet to show up on oil markets. While the price of crude has risen in the past week as the fragile Iran war ceasefire has crumbled, the remarkably restrained price moves have helped create a false sense of security.

items.title: Oil-rich Gulf nations building alternatives to Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Battery installation rates collapse as 'solar-coaster' strikes back
items.summary: Federal subsidies have ignited huge demand for batteries, but a tightening of the rules has experts warning of "boom and bust" cycles for the industry.

items.title: Australia's newest medical drama The F Ward, starring Ioane Sa'ula, mixes Bump with Grey's Anatomy
items.summary: Growing up, Ioane Sa'ula thought he'd probably play rugby league professionally, but an injury scuppered his plans. Now, he's the romantic lead of new Aussie medical drama, The F Ward.

items.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Sam Neill's Marvel cameo, 70s punk rock and numbat tongues
items.summary: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers dies aged 89
items.summary: Sir Don Bradman once described Sobers as "the greatest all-round cricketer I ever saw".

items.title: Lao authorities charge distillery owners over fatal methanol poisoning, Danish officials confirm
items.summary: Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles, both 19, died after the incident at the Nana Backpackers Hostel in Vang Vieng in November 2024.

items.title: Star Swan takes MOTY contender as Crows notch vital win
items.summary: The Adelaide Crows beat the Swans in a thriller in the wet at the SCG to jump into the top four.

items.title: Stepson sentenced to nine years in jail over mummified body found in Gold Coast family home
items.summary: Nikola Golem lied to his "mentally unwell" mother about her husband's death for more than a year, and let her sleep beside the decomposing body, Brisbane's Supreme Court heard.

items.title: Mount Barker developer and restaurant 'shocked' by compulsory acquisition
items.summary: Aston Hills Mount Barker developers and a popular restaurant say their investments have been turned upside down by a state government compulsory acquisition announced this week.

items.title: Mount Gambier man admits to indecent behaviour at third shop in April
items.summary: A Mount Gambier man who previously admitted going naked in two local food businesses pleads guilty to another charge of indecent behaviour, as his case heads to the new South Australian Veterans Court.

items.summary: Will Warbrick's Melbourne Storm career appears to be over, with the departing winger rupturing his Achilles in his side's 14-6 loss to the Sydney Roosters.

items.title: Stockland's Halls Creek housing development fast-tracked by Queensland government
items.summary: The Queensland government declares the Halls Creek development a priority development area. The project is expected to deliver 12,000 new homes.

items.title: New report lists Melbourne and Sydney as world's most expensive cities for beer and cigarettes
items.summary: The Mapping the World's Prices report, published by Deutsche Bank, compares cost-of-living data in 69 cities across six continents.

items.summary: Days after a migratory bird tested positive for bird flu in Wellington, authorities have detected a second case of the deadly virus in a native bird.

items.title: Donald Trump's failure to let go of 2020 an 'ominous sign' ahead of upcoming midterm elections
items.summary: Using a prime-time address to the nation to repeat long-debunked claims about the 2020 election defeat is a desperate and unusual move. Analysts say it's an "ominous sign" of how Donald Trump may handle the upcoming midterms.

items.title: Royal commissions might 'fund lawyers' but violence experts want to fund solutions
items.summary: Experts are divided about whether there should be a royal commission into violence against women but say the solutions to the problem are already known and need funding.

items.title: Bus driver found guilty over Brisbane CBD crash that killed Tia Cameron appeals conviction
items.summary: Lindsay Francis Selby was found guilty last year of driving without due care and attention when he struck and killed Tia Cameron in 2024.

items.title: What's living in your make-up brushes and is it harmful?

items.title: Supreme Court battle over traditional owner consultation pauses flood works at Nyah
items.summary: The Victorian government and a regional water authority have admitted they did not property consult with traditional owners before approving a controversial flood plain restoration project.

items.title: University standards body raised concerns about violent outsiders taking part in campus protests

items.title: How the Hawks are making their two-ruck strategy work in the 2026 AFL season
items.summary: Once thought to be dead and buried, the era of the dual ruck has been resuscitated by a handful of clubs in finals contention, including Hawthorn.

items.title: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says AI central to raising productivity and lowering interest rates
items.summary: The Treasurer has given an insight into his April meeting with Anthropic, arguing that Australia embracing AI is crucial for turning around the nation's lacklustre productivity and economic performance over recent decades.

items.title: Trump rails against US voting systems in prime-time speech ahead of critical midterms
items.summary: US President Donald Trump uses a presidential speech to accuse China of improperly trying to influence the 2020 election and stop him from winning the presidency.

items.title: Mount Isa Police allegedly seize $165,000 worth of methamphetamine from outback home
items.summary: Police allege more than 200 grams of methamphetamines have been seized during a drug bust at a Mount Isa home in outback Queensland.

items.title: Josh Fawaz's Like a Prayer now has AI credits on Spotify
items.summary: DJ and music producer Josh Fawaz has updated the song credits on Spotify on his viral track Like a Prayer. Generative AI drums and vocals are now credited.

items.summary: Former professional cyclist and two-time Olympian Rohan Dennis pleads guilty after he was caught driving while disqualified, not far from where his wife died two-and-a-half years ago.

items.title: Smoke from Canada wildfires crosses into US prompting warnings to stay indoors

items.title: Damien Leith reflects on friendship with Roy Orbison's co-songwriter

items.title: From sports comedies to Coober Pedy mystery, here's what to watch in July
items.summary: Plus a noir mystery set in outback Coober Pedy, a true crime drama which doesn't exploit its victims, and more new TV to watch this month.

items.title: Ancient trade fast evaporating as distillers call for coopering skills to be better preserved
items.summary: This ancient trade of coopering is fast evaporating as distillers look abroad to help keep their flavours top shelf.

items.title: Daryl Maguire found guilty of conspiracy over fraudulent visa scheme
items.summary: After five days of deliberation in the NSW District Court the former Member for Wagga Wagga was found to have conspired to provide false or misleading information to the Commonwealth on visas for Chinese nationals seeking permanent residency.

items.title: Destructive cyclone season has communities in northern WA still cleaning up
items.summary: Two cyclones in less than a month caused significant damage to the Dampier Peninsula in the Kimberley region during the 2025-26 wet season. Months later, residents say costly repairs are continuing.

items.title: Trump claims China acquired personal information of 220m US voters — as it happened
items.summary: Donald Trump claims declassified documents reveal China carried out the "largest compromise of election data in history".

items.title: Central Queensland residents warn of fire risks on overgrown crown land
items.summary: A central Queensland man is concerned his neighbour's overgrown plot is a fire hazard. His neighbour is the state government.

items.summary: Three men are charged in separate Launceston suburbs over an alleged violent armed robbery and house fire at Coles Bay, on Tasmania's east coast.

items.title: Ngura Kutju set up in Alice Springs to advocate for Indigenous families in NPY Lands
items.summary: A new hub to support families caught between three child protection agencies has been established in Alice Springs in a 12-month trial.

items.title: Groundwater concerns grow as state pushes ahead with Taroom Trough oil plans
